title: Per Kørner

infobox.value: (26 April 1936 – 27 September 2018)[[1]](#cite_note-1) was a Norwegian priest and [anti-abortion activist](/wiki/Anti-abortion_activist "Anti-abortion activist").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Norwegian priest and anti-abortion activist

**Per Kørner** (26 April 1936 – 27 September 2018)[[1]](#cite_note-1) was a Norwegian priest and [anti-abortion activist](/wiki/Anti-abortion_activist "Anti-abortion activist").

Kørner was a missionary priest in [South Africa](/wiki/South_Africa "South Africa") from 1966 to 1978, and parish priest at [Strandebarm Church](/wiki/Strandebarm_Church "Strandebarm Church") from 1981 to 1991. He broke with the state governance of the [Church of Norway](/wiki/Church_of_Norway "Church of Norway") in 1984 in solidarity with anti-abortion priest [Børre Knudsen](/wiki/B%C3%B8rre_Knudsen "Børre Knudsen"), and was defrocked by a court ruling in 1991. He then continued as priest in the independent [Deanery of Strandebarm](/wiki/Deanery_of_Strandebarm "Deanery of Strandebarm") (also known as the "Church of Norway in Exile") along with Knudsen.[[2]](#cite_note-idag-2)

He joined the political party [Abortion Opponents' List](/wiki/Abortion_Opponents%27_List "Abortion Opponents' List") as deputy leader for the 2009 parliamentary election alongside [Ludvig Nessa](/wiki/Ludvig_Nessa "Ludvig Nessa") and [Ivar Kristianslund](/wiki/Ivar_Kristianslund "Ivar Kristianslund") from the "exile church".[[3]](#cite_note-3) He has later protested against abortion and homosexuality during Church of Norway church services, kneeling in front of the church altar in the middle of church services, including in the [Bergen Cathedral](/wiki/Bergen_Cathedral "Bergen Cathedral"), the [St John's Church, Bergen](/wiki/St_John%27s_Church,_Bergen "St John's Church, Bergen"), and the [Tromsø Cathedral](/wiki/Troms%C3%B8_Cathedral "Tromsø Cathedral"), when King [Harald V](/wiki/Harald_V "Harald V") has been present for the investiture of new bishops.[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)

Kørner resigned from his church services in 2016 due to cancer,[[2]](#cite_note-idag-2) and died in 2018.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

3. **[^](#cite_ref-3)** ["Abortmotstandere starter nytt parti"](http://www.vg.no/nyheter/innenriks/abortmotstandere-starter-nytt-parti/a/534410/). *Verdens Gang* (in Norwegian). NTB. 19 August 2008.
4. **[^](#cite_ref-4)** Hansen, Birthe Steen (8 March 2009). ["Abortstunt foran Kongen i kirken"](https://www.nettavisen.no/per-korner/abort/kong-harald/abortstunt-foran-kongen-i-kirken/s/12-95-2568763). *Nettavisen* (in Norwegian).
5. **[^](#cite_ref-5)** Rydland, Sølve (6 May 2013). ["Prest kasta ut frå homo-gudsteneste"](https://www.nrk.no/vestland/prest-kasta-ut-fra-homo-gudsteneste-1.11014502). *NRK* (in Norwegian).
6. **[^](#cite_ref-6)** Haaland, Joakim Dahl (9 November 2014). ["Abortmotstander Per Kørner aksjonerte under kongegudstjeneste"](https://www.tv2.no/nyheter/innenriks/abortmotstander-per-korner-aksjonerte-under-kongegudstjeneste/6214693/). *TV 2* (in Norwegian).
